name: Understanding-first
summary: Before building anything substantive, write a restatement of the request (goal + concrete steps + assumptions) to a file the operator can read, and let them confirm before work proceeds.
evidence: Caught several misreads at zero cost — a panel confirmation is cheaper than a wrong build.
Now a standing CLAUDE.md convention (understanding.md + the Understanding panel).

name: Build-less local app
summary: Ship a UI as a self-contained folder of static assets (index.html + JS/CSS + vendored libs + data) served under `/api/localview/{repo}/app/{appId}/`. No build step, no node_modules, no CDN.
evidence: homepage/, the Understanding app, and this Lab all use it.
Zero build tooling to break; full git history of the content; instant edits.

name: Synthetic harness app
summary: A `kind:harness` local app that the harness serves internally with repo context, instead of dialing a loopback port. It appears in the app switcher like any local app but needs no running process.
evidence: The always-on Understanding app proved it.
This Lab reuses the exact mechanism (HarnessStaticApp.Serve), scoped to the self repo.

name: Shared component, two surfaces
summary: When the same UI must appear in two places (e.g. a routed tab and a dashboard dock), extract ONE component both render — so the surfaces can never drift apart.
evidence: AutopilotConsole (tab + dock), FilesBrowser (Files tab + dock), IdeasPanel (Ideas tab + dashboard).
Each was a correction after an earlier duplicated implementation drifted.

name: Relative URLs only (under a proxy sub-path)
summary: Inside a proxied app, reference assets with relative URLs (`./app.js`), never a leading slash. A leading slash escapes the `/api/localview/<repo>/app/<id>/` sub-path and 404s.
evidence: Codified in docs/local-exposure-convention.md and the homepage's first topic.
Every build-less app here follows it; violations fail loudly (404), not silently.

name: Isolated preview verification
summary: Before a disruptive change to the live harness, run the new build on a throwaway port with an isolated data dir, confirm the exact behaviour, THEN swap `:5099`.
evidence: Repeatedly caught issues before they reached the operator's live session.
Used today to confirm the Understanding-app fix before restarting :5099.

name: Operator-side gate for risky capability
summary: For any capability that can *act* (e.g. autopilot sending prompts), the web UI may see and shrink it but can never enable it. Only the host (WinForms) flips it on. Default off.
evidence: Interim guard shipped (autopilot endpoints gated host-side, default off).
Still under test: the full mitigation (a scoped capability token held by the engine, not the brain) is NOT built yet.
